title: rubus sparsiflorus
intro: Rubus sparsiflorus, also known as the thimbleberry, is a type of bramble shrub native to western North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Rubus sparsiflorus include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is also used in culinary applications, such as making jams and preserves.
